AuthorDate: Fri May 29 11:39:05 2020 +0800
Commit:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
CommitDate: Sun Jul 5 13:01:36 2020 +0200

    avformat/hls: check segment duration value of EXTINF
    
    fix ticket: 8673
    set the default EXTINF duration to 1ms if duration is smaller than 1ms

commit 0c543e3f5b3881d515d6235f152aacaaaf3aba72
Author:     Yann Ylavic <ylavic@apache.org>
AuthorDate: Fri Jul 24 09:35:25 2020 +0000
Commit:     Yann Ylavic <ylavic@apache.org>
CommitDate: Fri Jul 24 09:35:25 2020 +0000

    Merge r1880205, r1880214 from trunk:
    
    mod_proxy_uwsgi: Error out on HTTP header larger than 16K
    
    The uwsgi protocol does not let us serialize more than 16K of HTTP header,
    so fail early with 500 if it happens.
    
    
    Follow up to r1880205, APLOGN

    of coherency for SEV encrypted memory,

    reclaim also includes it being unmapped from KVM's memslots).  Due to lack
    of coherency for SEV encrypted memory,

Author:     Taehee Yoo <ap420073@gmail.com>
AuthorDate: Mon Jul 5 15:38:07 2021 +0000
Commit:     David S. Miller <davem@davemloft.net>
CommitDate: Tue Jul 6 10:36:59 2021 -0700

    bonding: fix null dereference in bond_ipsec_add_sa()
    
    If bond doesn't have real device, bond->curr_active_slave is null.
    But bond_ipsec_add_sa() dereferences bond->curr_active_slave without
    null checking.
    So, null-ptr-deref would occur.
    
    Tes

    Correctly compare EdiPartyName in GENERAL_NAME_cmp()
    
    If a GENERAL_NAME field contained EdiPartyName data then it was
    incorrectly being handled as type "other". This could lead to a
    segmentation fault.

Author:     Matt Caswell <matt@openssl.org>
AuthorDate: Wed Nov 11 16:12:58 2020 +0000
Commit:     Matt Caswell <matt@openssl.org>
CommitDate: Tue Dec 8 10:18:44 2020 +0000

    Correctly compare EdiPartyName in GENERAL_NAME_cmp()
    
    If a GENERAL_NAME field contained EdiPartyName data then it was
    incorrectly being handled as type "other". This could lead to a
    segmentation fault.
    
    Many thanks to David Benjamin from Google for r

commit ff0507c239a246fd7215b31c5658fc6a3ee1e4c5
Author:     Chen Qun <kuhn.chenqun@huawei.com>
AuthorDate: Sat Apr 18 14:26:02 2020 +0800
Commit:     Peter Maydell <peter.maydell@linaro.org>
CommitDate: Mon Apr 20 11:31:46 2020 +0100

    block/iscsi:fix heap-buffer-overflow in iscsi_aio_ioctl_cb
    
    There is an overflow, the source 'datain.data[2]' is 100 bytes,
     but the 'ss' is 252 bytes.This may cause a security issue because
     we can access a lot of unrelated memory data.

    stack.
